functions.name: split_datalawyer.sentences.split_utils.multi_replace
documentation:
Given a string and a dict, replaces occurrences of the dict keys found in the
string, with their corresponding values. The replacements will occur in "one pass",
i.e. there should be no clashes.
\:param str string: string to perform replacements on
\:param dict replacements: replacement dictionary {str_to_find: str_to_replace_with}
\:param bool ignore_case: whether to ignore case when looking for matches
\:rtype: str the replaced string

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.pdf_utils.clean_text
documentation: Apply all regex above to a given string.

modules.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils
documentation:
Utilities for working with the local dataset cache.

From https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/allennlp/common/file_utils.py

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils.url_to_filename
documentation:
Convert `url` into a hashed filename in a repeatable way.
If `etag` is specified, append its hash to the url's, delimited
by a period.

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils.filename_to_url
documentation:
Return the url and etag (which may be `None`) stored for `filename`.
Raise `FileNotFoundError` if `filename` or its stored metadata do not exist.

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils.cached_path
documentation:
Given something that might be a URL (or might be a local path),
determine which. If it's a URL, download the file and cache it, and
return the path to the cached file. If it's already a local path,
make sure the file exists and then return the path.

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils.is_url_or_existing_file
documentation:
Given something that might be a URL (or might be a local path),
determine check if it's url or an existing file path.

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils._split_s3_path
documentation: Split a full s3 path into the bucket name and path.

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils._s3_request
documentation:
Wrapper function for s3 requests in order to create more helpful error
messages.

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils._s3_get
documentation: Pull a file directly from S3.

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils._session_with_backoff
documentation:
We ran into an issue where http requests to s3 were timing out,
possibly because we were making too many requests too quickly.
This helper function returns a requests session that has retry-with-backoff
built in. See
<https://stackoverflow.com/questions/23267409/how-to-implement-retry-mechanism-into-python-requests-library>.

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils.get_from_cache
documentation:
Given a URL, look for the corresponding dataset in the local cache.
If it's not there, download it. Then return the path to the cached file.
TODO(joelgrus): do we want to do checksums or anything like that?

functions.name: split_datalawyer.utils.file_utils.read_set_from_file
documentation:
Extract a de-duped collection (set) of text from a file.
Expected file format is one item per line.
